description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and rounded edges, featuring a polished wood grain finish. The front panel displays a classic analog tuning scale with frequency markings, flanked by two large rotary knobs for tuning and volume control, and a power button on the left. Two telescopic metal antennas extend from the back, suggesting AM/FM reception capability. The sides of the radio have dark speaker grilles, while the rear panel includes cable connections and a metal plate, likely for power and external antenna input. The overall design is reminiscent of mid-20th-century home electronics, combining both functional and decorative elements. Its size and construction indicate it is intended for tabletop or shelf placement in an urban environment.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with telescopic antennas, analog tuning scale, and rotary knobs, featuring side speaker grilles and a polished finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the analog tuning scale, two large rotary knobs, a power button, and the extended antennas.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the wooden finish and the dark speaker grille, with antennas visible extending from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the two telescopic antennas, cable connections, and a metal plate, with the wooden casing prominent.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the wooden finish and speaker grille, with cables and antennas extending from the back.
